title: Queenstown 10-Day Adventure Tour
metaTitle: Queenstown 10-Day Adventure Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 10-day trip to Queenstown, New Zealand: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Skyline Gondola & Luge and Kawarau Bridge Bungy. Prices from $2789 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 10 days discovering Queenstown, New Zealand — Alpine climate with cold, snowy winters (Jun-Aug) suited to skiing, and mild, long-daylight summers (Dec-Feb) suited to hiking and adventure sports; seasons are reversed from the Northern Hemisphere. June to September for ski season, or December to February for summer hiking, bungy jumping and long daylight hours. This itinerary blends Skyline Gondola & Luge, Kawarau Bridge Bungy, Queenstown Gardens with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
9 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Skyline Gondola & Luge, Kawarau Bridge Bungy, Queenstown Gardens
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Queenstown
itinerary.description: Land at Queenstown Airport (ZQN) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Skyline Gondola & Luge — Cable car with panoramic views and luge track. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Queenstown: Kawarau Bridge Bungy & Shotover Jet
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Kawarau Bridge Bungy — The original commercial bungy jump site. In the afternoon, continue to Shotover Jet — High-speed jet boat through Shotover Canyon. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Queenstown: Nevis Bungy & Milford Sound day cruise (from Queenstown)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Nevis Bungy — One of the highest bungy jumps in the world. In the afternoon, continue to Milford Sound day cruise (from Queenstown) — Full-day trip including fiord cruise. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Milford Sound
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Milford Sound, a popular side trip from Queenstown. Return to Queenstown in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Moke Lake — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Queenstown: TSS Earnslaw steamship cruise & Queenstown Ziptrek Ecotours
itinerary.description: Morning visit to TSS Earnslaw steamship cruise — Vintage steamship to Walter Peak High Country Farm. In the afternoon, continue to Queenstown Ziptrek Ecotours — Zipline canopy tour above the gondola. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Glenorchy
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Glenorchy, a popular side trip from Queenstown. Return to Queenstown in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Queenstown: Skydive Queenstown & Gibbston Valley wine tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Skydive Queenstown — Tandem skydive over Lake Wakatipu and the Remarkables. In the afternoon, continue to Gibbston Valley wine tour — Guided tasting tour of the wine trail. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Glenorchy Lord of the Rings filming locations — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Glenorchy Lord of the Rings filming locations

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Routeburn Track guided day walk or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to ZQN for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book bungy, jet boat, and skydive activities in advance during peak season
Milford Sound involves a long drive — consider flying in or staying overnight
Check mountain weather forecasts before any hike, as conditions change fast
Sort your NZeTA and levy payment online before you fly
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Layered thermal clothing for rapid alpine weather changes
Sturdy hiking or ski boots depending on season
Sunglasses and high-SPF sunscreen for intense alpine UV
thingsToDo: Skiing at Coronet Peak or The Remarkables
thingsToAvoid: Underestimating how quickly alpine weather can turn on exposed trails
Driving unfamiliar mountain roads at night without chains in winter
Skipping travel insurance that covers adventure sports
Overpacking your day with activities and no weather-delay buffer
topRestaurants: Rata — Modern New Zealand by chef Josh Emett ($$$)
Amisfield Winery Restaurant — New Zealand degustation ($$$)
instagramSpots: Lake Wakatipu with The Remarkables backdrop
hiddenGems: Glenorchy Lord of the Rings filming locations
familyActivities: Queenstown Gardens disc golf and playgrounds
adventureActivities: Skiing at Coronet Peak or The Remarkables
luxuryExperiences: Helicopter tour with a glacier picnic landing
Luxury lodge stay overlooking Lake Wakatipu
honeymoonExperiences: Private helicopter flight over Milford Sound
Couples spa retreat with mountain views
budgetExperiences: Hike Queenstown Hill for panoramic views

festivalCalendar.name: Lake Wanaka Festival of Colour (nearby, biennial)

localEtiquette: Respect Māori culture, language, and any protocols at cultural sites.
Tipping is not customary but is welcomed for outstanding service.
Sign intentions books before remote backcountry hikes for safety tracking.
Follow Leave No Trace principles in national parks and alpine areas.
Queue politely for gondolas, jet boats, and adventure activity check-ins.
localTransportGuide: Central Queenstown is walkable, but a rental car or the Orbus public bus network is useful for reaching Glenorchy, Arrowtown, and the ski fields. Shuttle services connect Queenstown Airport to accommodation, and ride-share apps operate in town.
bestTimeToVisit: June to September for ski season, or December to February for summer hiking, bungy jumping and long daylight hours.
weather: Alpine climate with cold, snowy winters (Jun-Aug) suited to skiing, and mild, long-daylight summers (Dec-Feb) suited to hiking and adventure sports; seasons are reversed from the Northern Hemisphere.
visaInformation: Most visa-waiver nationalities (US, UK, EU, Canada) must apply online for an NZeTA (New Zealand Electronic Travel Authority) before arrival and pay the International Visitor Conservation and Tourism Levy; Australian citizens are exempt.
averageFlightTime: ~17.6h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Queenstown?
faqs.answer: Most visa-waiver nationalities (US, UK, EU, Canada) must apply online for an NZeTA (New Zealand Electronic Travel Authority) before arrival and pay the International Visitor Conservation and Tourism Levy; Australian citizens are exempt.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Queenstown?
faqs.answer: June to September for ski season, or December to February for summer hiking, bungy jumping and long daylight hours. Alpine climate with cold, snowy winters (Jun-Aug) suited to skiing, and mild, long-daylight summers (Dec-Feb) suited to hiking and adventure sports; seasons are reversed from the Northern Hemisphere.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Queenstown safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Queenstown is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Queenstown?
faqs.answer: The local currency is NZD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Queenstown is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset TSS Earnslaw steamship cruise experience.

reviews.author: Elena Ibrahim
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond TSS Earnslaw steamship cruise and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Olivia Smith
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Our trip to Queenstown was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Queenstown Gardens was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.
